timeline.memex_content:
Recent timeline summary (rewritten memex)

Overview
- Source data: recent events from events.db and local project activity logs.
- Purpose: capture and index recent activity (opencode sessions and an automated synthesis run) and record actionable next steps for ingestion and QA.

Key entries (canonicalized)
1) synthesis.start — 2026-03-22T04:10:57.639323Z (local: Sat Mar 21, 9:10 PM PDT)
   - Source: syke
   - Event: synthesis.start initiated
   - Note: Start of an automated synthesis run. Treat this as a high-level anchor for associating outputs from the following opencode sessions.

2) opencode session.start series — 2026-01-17 (multiple short sessions)
   - Source: opencode
   - Project: ~/Documents/InnerNets AI Inc/persona/persona
   - Sessions & timestamps (local times):
     • ses_4326bf609ffeXGhu5dUJboC2aL — 2026-01-17T20:09:49.814000Z → 2026-01-20T07:42:09.627000Z (long session spanning Jan 17–20)
     • ses_4324a0e3dffepwbKP778jDxk6K — 2026-01-17T20:46:51.842000Z → 2026-01-17T20:47:55.187000Z
     • ses_43249ff53ffe7ITJ7sYn4i7uWV — 2026-01-17T20:46:55.660000Z → 2026-01-17T20:47:27.173000Z
     • ses_43249f010ffe4b5mMpxoaujifG — 2026-01-17T20:46:59.567000Z → 2026-01-17T20:47:27.856000Z
     • ses_43249e341ffep6Unl2sthFE786 — 2026-01-17T20:47:02.846000Z → 2026-01-17T20:48:51.069000Z
     • ses_43249d6caffe5PZ3eXDtBz33VZ — 2026-01-17T20:47:06.037000Z → 2026-01-17T20:50:14.293000Z
     • ses_4324739d8ffehSf7F4AiOgzW62 — 2026-01-17T23:43:40.348000Z → 2026-01-17T23:44:26.651000Z
   - Common context: all sessions reference the same repo path (persona). Many are short bursts (~seconds–minutes) clustered around a local midday window; one session spans multiple days.

Implications / Actionable items
- Indexing
  • Index all opencode session events as coding-session evidence for the persona repository. Include session_id, start/end times, duration, and any available sequence_index or parent_event_id metadata.
  • Link the synthesis.start event as an overarching synthesis run and associate its output (if available) with these opencode sessions.

- Database and query guidance
  • DB file: /Users/saxenauts/Documents/personal/syke/experiments/runs/clean_zero/replay.db (events table schema: id, timestamp, source, event_type, title, role, model, content, stop_reason, input_tokens, output_tokens, session_id, sequence_index, parent_event_id, external_id, ingested_at, user_id)
  • Cursor (last processed event ID): 069b8965-bfe8-7c26-8000-80c115690d68 — use WHERE id > '<cursor>' to fetch pending events.
  • Helpful SQL examples:
    - Count by source for new events: SELECT source, COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-bfe8-7c26-8000-80c115690d68' GROUP BY source;
    - Top sessions by turns: SELECT session_id, COUNT(*) AS turns FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-bfe8-7c26-8000-80c115690d68' GROUP BY session_id ORDER BY turns DESC LIMIT 10;
    - Fetch full pending events: SELECT * FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-bfe8-7c26-8000-80c115690d68' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 500;

- Prioritization
  • There are 20 pending events since the last synthesis run — process these first. Verify whether any of them are duplicates or belong to the same session chains.
  • Prioritize events where source IN ('opencode','syke') and events that include session_id values tied to the persona repo.

- Tagging & linking
  • Tag all opencode sessions with the project path and a coding-session tag.
  • Link synthesis.start to the set of opencode session events (create a relationship in memory/metadata) so future queries can retrieve synthesis results alongside source sessions.

Next steps (recommended)
1. Run the SQL count/group queries above on replay.db to confirm the 20 pending events and see distribution by source and session.
2. Read the content field for opencode events and extract any file paths, diffs, or commit references to augment indexing.
3. Index events into the memex with structured fields: {id, timestamp, source, event_type, project_path, session_id, duration, content_summary}.
4. Attach the synthesis.start event as a parent/anchor for the recent synthesis cycle and link related sessions.
5. After indexing, update the processed-cursor to the maximum id consumed and re-run aggregate queries to ensure no remaining pending events.

Notes and constraints
- Keep an eye out for large content fields (content > several KB) — truncate summaries but preserve links to raw event rows.
- The synthesis.start timestamp is slightly later than the session timestamps; verify whether the synthesis was triggered after these sessions or corresponds to a different run.

Provenance
- Derived from the 'Current Document' timeline summary and the events.db schema and suggested queries provided in the runtime context.

Audit hints (for future cycles)
- When processing, log the SQL used and number of rows changed/ingested (for traceability).
- If any sessions have overlapping or nested parent_event_id values, preserve the parent-child links in the memory layer.

End of rewritten memex.

timeline.memex_content:
Recent timeline summary (processed)

Overview
- Source data: events table in /Users/saxenauts/Documents/personal/syke/experiments/runs/clean_zero/replay.db
- Purpose: consume and index pending events since the previous cursor and record actionable next steps for ingestion and QA.

What I ran

  1) SELECT source, COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2' GROUP BY source;
  2) SELECT session_id, COUNT(*) as turns FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2' GROUP BY session_id ORDER BY turns DESC LIMIT 20;
  3) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2'; SELECT MAX(id) FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2';

- Total pending events found: 44
- Event count by source: opencode: 34, syke: 8
- Top sessions (first 20 returned; most have 1 turn each):
  ses_42c2b9c32ffegrfRtDsfZyHVZN, ses_42c2b86eeffe0fcSlM0X0bto5v, ses_42c2b7543ffeGqgw1zHzPuL0q7, ses_42c2b730dffeVHhdzE4Z5zDiTq, ses_42c2b6d96ffeknbtzU26IjvWzE, ses_42c2b681cffeegi4agjc0Ix671, ses_42c2b6109ffeMmzZ31lJ8U0qbf, ses_42c2b5909ffeFEuIfcsMWnoO6x, ses_42c2b48bfffeeoj4O03xJc4bdO, ses_42c2af94effeTKgrDFmGobiIAg, ses_42c292b76ffeJ1IdUectEnznB6, ses_42c28bd40ffefG6euGjdcNCPFd, ses_42c289a29ffel4aEj4ryGPJskf, ses_42c28087effecVhzGe0TZKpGV4, ses_427dbf540ffepTgfstI7Zx0rjp, ses_427dbd9e0ffesDv4llg4jubKGD, ses_427dbc219ffe3JrjCahesDuehN, ses_427cb33beffeLGpAbLkIsv3L3d, ses_427cb1ca9ffezPJv4UzzlM4In1
- Highest event id among pending rows (new cursor candidate): 069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555

Implications / Actions taken (recommended)
- Indexing
  • Tag and index all opencode events as coding-session evidence for the persona repository. For each event include: id, timestamp, source, event_type, project_path (if present), session_id, duration (if start/end available), sequence_index/parent_event_id.
  • Tag syke events and link any syke synthesis.start events as anchors for associating outputs with opencode sessions.

- Cursor and ingestion
  • Set the processed-cursor to the MAX(id) consumed: 069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555 after successful indexing of the 44 pending events.
  • Use WHERE id > '<old_cursor>' to fetch pending events in future runs; verify no duplicates by checking parent_event_id and external_id fields.

- Prioritization
  • Prioritize processing events where source IN ('opencode','syke') and those with session_id values tied to the persona repo. There are 34 opencode events to ingest first, then 8 syke events.

- Tagging & linking
  • Tag opencode sessions with the project path and a coding-session tag. Preserve parent_event_id relationships where present.
  • Link synthesis.start events (syke) to the set of opencode session events to enable retrieval of synthesis outputs alongside their source sessions.

Next steps (recommended)
1) Fetch full pending rows: SELECT * FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 500; read content fields and extract file paths, diffs, or commit references.
2) Index events into memex with structured fields: {id, timestamp, source, event_type, project_path, session_id, duration, content_summary}. Truncate large content but keep link to raw row.
3) Link syke synthesis.start events as parents/anchors for related opencode sessions.
4) Update processed-cursor to 069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555 and re-run aggregate queries to ensure no remaining pending events.

Notes and constraints
- Watch for large content fields (content > several KB) — summarize but keep pointer to raw event row.
- If any sessions have overlapping or nested parent_event_id values, preserve the parent-child linkage in the memory layer.

Provenance / Audit trail
- Logged SQL used (see "What I ran").
- Counts and top session IDs derived directly from replay.db queries.

Hints for future cycles
- Consider batching content reads for sessions with many small events to reduce DB round trips.
- When indexing, store both a short summary and a link to the raw event row to allow full retrieval if needed.

timeline.memex_content:
Timeline ingestion cycle — processed pending events from replay.db (cursor: > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2')

Summary
- Found 44 pending events (query window id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2').
- Sources: opencode = 34, syke = 8.
- Top session IDs (example set): ses_42c2b9c32ffegrfRtDsfZyHVZN, ses_42c2b86eeffe0fcSlM0X0bto5v, ses_42c2b7543ffeGqgw1zHzPuL0q7, ... (most sessions have 1 turn).
- Highest pending event id (new cursor candidate): 069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555.

Indexing / Tagging recommendations
- Treat all opencode events as coding-session evidence for the persona repository. Record fields: id, timestamp, source, event_type, project_path (if present), session_id, duration (if start/end), sequence_index, parent_event_id, content pointer to raw row.
- Tag opencode sessions with project_path and coding-session tag; preserve parent_event_id relationships.
- Tag syke events; link syke synthesis.start events as anchors for related opencode session outputs.
- For large content (>few KB) store a short content_summary and pointer to raw event row to enable retrieval.

Cursor & ingestion policy
- After successful indexing, set processed-cursor to the MAX(id) consumed: 069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555.
- Fetch pending events using: SELECT * FROM events WHERE id > '<last_cursor>' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 500; (read content and extract file paths, diffs, commit refs).
- Verify duplicates by checking parent_event_id and external_id before double-indexing.

Prioritization
- Ingest opencode (34 rows) first, then syke (8 rows).
- Prioritize sessions tied to persona repo; batch small events per session to reduce DB round trips.

Next actionable steps (to execute now)
1) Fetch full pending rows and extract structured metadata: file paths, diffs, commit refs, project_path. SQL: SELECT * FROM events WHERE id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 500;
2) Index into memex with fields: {id, timestamp, source, event_type, project_path, session_id, duration, sequence_index, parent_event_id, content_summary, raw_row_link}.
3) Create links: For each syke synthesis.start, find opencode events with matching session_id/time window and link them as synthesis anchors.
4) Update processed-cursor to '069bf6c2-629d-7d5d-8000-731ec2a62555' after indexing completes and re-run aggregate queries to confirm no remaining pending events.

QA / Watchouts
- Summarize large content and keep pointer to raw row for full retrieval.
- Preserve parent-child linkage for nested parent_event_id values.
- Avoid indexing duplicates: dedupe by event id, parent_event_id, external_id.

Provenance
- SQL queries used and counts derived directly from /Users/saxenauts/Documents/personal/syke/experiments/runs/clean_zero/replay.db.
- This cycle processed events with id > '069b8965-c007-7eae-8000-3da264638cc2'.

Hints for future cycles
- Batch small events by session to reduce DB round trips.
- Store both short summary and raw row link for long content.
- Consider running a lightweight pre-scan to detect sessions with many events and batch-read them.

Audit
- Logged queries and counts are available in run history. Recommend storing this cycle summary with the memex entry for auditing.
